signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Options + Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 158.81 — President Trump's cancellation of a planned attack on Iran has eased immediate safe-haven demand, yet 300bp Fed-BoJ carry sustains the structural bid. Options market remains heavily skewed toward downside protection with 25d RR at -1.66 (strong put skew) as Japan's Finance Minister Katayama warns of speculative moves. JPY COT LF net short -70,605 confirms a crowded carry trade vulnerable to verbal or physical intervention as price tests the 160.00 threshold.

signals.title: GBP/AUD — G8 Bilateral Theme + Carry Divergence
signals.text: GBP/AUD G8 spread at 0.928% identifies a dominant bilateral theme as AUD underperforms peers by -0.577%. Despite a -60bp carry disadvantage for GBP (BoE 3.75% vs RBA 4.35%), GBP/USD strength and easing Middle East tensions are weighing on the high-beta AUD. COT data shows AUD LF net long +55,794 (74.8% long), suggesting a crowded long position at risk if RBA minutes fail to reinforce hiking bias.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Volatility + Positioning + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7172 — Options market pricing reflects extreme hedging demand with IV at 21.3% vs HV30 of 10.4%, creating a massive +10.8% Vol Risk Premium. While COT LF specs remain net long +55,794, retail sentiment is 72% short, creating a sharp institutional-retail divergence. The elevated VRP suggests the market is paying a high premium for protection against a sharp break, likely tied to the 25d RR put skew of -1.03.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -19,437 (long% 22.3%) shows speculators fading the RBNZ despite a market bias for a hike at the next meeting. With NZD/USD at 0.5877 and retail 77% long, a hawkish RBNZ delivery on 27 May poses a significant short-squeeze risk for institutional accounts. Fed 3.75% vs RBNZ 2.25% provides a +150bp carry cushion for USD, but the crowded spec short remains the primary tactical risk.
